## Catch secrets before they leak into Cursor & Claude

Visit website

HeimWall image number 11

HeimWall image number 21

HeimWall image number 31

HeimWall catches leaked secrets, credentials, and PII the moment they're about to reach AI coding assistants like Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot, without blocking your workflow or uploading your prompts.

It's a lightweight macOS app that runs entirely on-device: 47 hand-written detection rules flag leaks in real time, in milliseconds. Your prompts never leave your Mac. No content stored, no account, no signup.

Free for individual engineers. Built so you stop pasting live API keys into AI tools without changing how you work. [...] Next up: a team dashboard that shows security leads leak trends across the org without ever exposing what anyone actually typed. Managers see signal, not content. We're onboarding design partners now, come say hi in the comments

results.title: The free Mac app | HeimWall
results.content:
Skip to content

HeimWall

For engineersFor teams

Free Mac app · 100% on-device

# Catch the secretbefore it leaves your Mac.

The free menu-bar app for individual developers. It flags secrets and PII in your AI prompts — in Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot — entirely on your Mac. No account, no org, nothing sent anywhere.

Get the free appHow it works

The whole point

This app sends nothing, anywhere. There is no account to create and no server to phone home to. Capture, detection, and the alert all happen on your Mac. Pull the network cable and it works exactly the same.

What it catches

## The stuff you never meant to paste.

One accidental paste from a .env file or a log is all it takes. The app watches the prompt as you write it and flags the things that shouldn’t go out. [...] ### Secrets, before you send them

AWS access keys, GitHub tokens, private keys, JWTs, database URLs — 25+ hand-written rules for the credentials with hard shapes. The most common way a secret enters a prompt is a paste from a .env file; this catches it at the source.

### PII you didn't mean to paste

SSNs, credit-card numbers, and emails buried in a log or a stack trace you dropped into Cursor to debug. Flagged instantly, before the prompt is sent.

### Across every AI tool you use

Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot — one menu-bar agent reads the composer itself, so it sees the prompt even when the tool talks straight to its own cloud. No per-tool setup. [...] The heavy lifting is deterministic regex — instant, explainable, and offline. A small pretrained classifier runs on-device as a fallback for fuzzier leaks. Either way, no model is ever trained on your prompts, because they are never collected.

Local-only, and we mean it

## Nothing leaves your Mac.

Not the prompt, not a redacted snippet, not a count. The entire loop lives on your machine — this is a claim we’ve audited, not a slogan.

Your machine — on-device

Step 1

Capture

Reads the prompt as you write it — on-device, before the network.

Step 2

Detect

Scans for secrets and PII in under 50ms, entirely offline.

Step 3

Flag

A soft toast at the source. Nothing is stored, nothing is sent.

prompt scantoastdropped from memory

No account, no cloud

results.title: GitHub - acunningham-ship-it/leakproof: Local secret firewall for AI coding assistants (Claude Code/Cursor/aider) — scans + redacts secrets before anything leaves the machine. Local-first, zero cloud. MIT. · GitHub
results.content:
Two ways to run it:

## Who it's for

Compliance-bound teams under SOC 2 / HIPAA / ITAR / GDPR whose security team blocked AI coding tools because the tools exfiltrate working-tree context — including any secrets in open files — to a cloud API. leakproof is the local technical control and audit trail that satisfies the objection.

The alternative tools (GitGuardian's ggshield recently added Claude Code and Cursor hooks) require a cloud account: scan metadata leaves the machine. That's structurally off the table for the shops that most need this. leakproof has zero cloud dependency — no account, no API key, no telemetry, nothing leaves the building.

## What it catches [...] The honest framing: leakproof catches what keyword scanners miss when the variable name is benign. The local-model semantic pass is opt-in and additive — you get the full regex+entropy layer with or without it.

## Install

Python 3.10+. The proxy surface needs `aiohttp`:

`aiohttp`

Prefer the bleeding edge? Install straight from the repo — the source is the package:

## How it works

`leakproof run -- claude` sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (or `OPENAI_API_BASE` for aider) to a local proxy on `127.0.0.1:8747`, then launches the tool. The proxy reads each request body, runs the scanner, forwards a redacted copy upstream, and streams the response back untouched. No certificate to install, no system-wide proxy, no interception of anything you didn't ask it to wrap. [...] Local-first secret firewall for AI coding assistants.

Your security team banned Claude Code or Cursor over data egress. Here's the local technical control that lets you turn them back on.

leakproof sits between the tool and the model API and reads every outbound request before it leaves the machine. Finds a secret, it redacts it or kills the request. Nothing hits the cloud. The decision happens on your laptop, which is the only setup that isn't self-defeating — you don't hand a key to a stranger to ask them whether it's a key.

results.title: FAQ | HeimWall
results.content:
How does HeimWall see prompts if it doesn't read them?

The agent sits locally alongside Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, and Windsurf. When you submit a prompt, the agent inspects it in-memory on your own machine, applies rule + ML detection, produces a classification, and emits redacted metadata (no body) to the dashboard. The original text is never written to disk by HeimWall and never transmitted to our cloud by default.

Is HeimWall data used for performance reviews?

No. By contract. Every customer MSA contains a clause prohibiting use of safety scores, flag history, or any HeimWall-derived signal in performance reviews, promotion decisions, or compensation decisions. The dashboard carries a non-dismissible banner on every page restating this. Violations are a breach of contract. [...] Skip to content

HeimWall

Join waitlist

# Short answers to honest questions.

Can't find something? Emailfounders@heimwall.ai. A human will answer.

## Product

What HeimWall does, where it runs, who it supports.

Which tools does HeimWall support today?

Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot, and Windsurf at launch. We instrument the local app. No browser extension, no proxy. When a new agentic tool hits critical mass, adding coverage is usually a one-week release because the detection engine is shared across tools.

Does HeimWall work on Windows or Linux? [...] Does HeimWall work on Windows or Linux?

macOS is first-class from day one (Apple Silicon and Intel, macOS 13+). Windows 11 support lands in Hafta 15 of our roadmap. Linux is post Demo Day. The engineering priority goes to whichever OS your team is actually running Cursor on, and today that answer is overwhelmingly macOS.

What's the detection latency?

p95 under 50 milliseconds on-device, measured on an M1 MacBook Air. The rule engine runs synchronously before the prompt leaves the process. The ML classifier runs in parallel and adds soft signal without blocking.

What happens if the HeimWall agent crashes?
